'''Graham Holroyd''' (born 25 October 1975) is an English [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer who played for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and [[Oldham RLFC]] as a {{rlp|so}}. He re-joined Halifax after a two-year spell with [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]]. He won the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] with Halifax in 2010, beating [[Featherstone Rovers]] 23–22.

==Playing career==
===Club career===
A former junior at [[Siddal A.R.L.F.C.|Siddal]], Holroyd made his professional [[rugby league]] debut for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]].<ref>{{cite web |title=1992-1993 |url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/timeline/1990s/1992-1993 |website=Leeds Rhinos |access-date=18 May 2024}}</ref> He played for Leeds in their [[Challenge Cup]] final defeats against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in [[1993–94 Challenge Cup|1994]] and [[1994–95 Challenge Cup|1995]], and also started in the heavy defeat against Wigan in the [[1994–95 Rugby League Premiership|1994–95 Premiership]] final at [[Old Trafford]]. He was a substitute for Leeds in the inaugural [[1998 Super League Grand Final]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Leeds Rhinos Nostalgia: Leeds lose out in first Grand Final in 1998 |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-nostalgia-leeds-lose-out-in-first-grand-final-in-1998-239593 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |access-date=18 May 2024 |date=22 October 2018}}</ref>

===Representative honours===
In 1998, he played for [[England national rugby league team|Emerging England]] in a 15–12 victory over Wales.<ref>{{cite news|last=Irvine|first=Christopher|title=Elder statesmen negotiate England escape|newspaper=The Times|date=20 July 1998|page=32|publisher=News International Trading Limited|location=London|id={{ProQuest|317976921}}}}</ref> He had been named in the [[Ireland national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Training Squad|Ireland training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] but was not included in the final side due to a knee injury sustained in training.<ref name="Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|title=Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad|publisher=Rugby League Ireland|date=1 August 2008|access-date=2 August 2008|archive-date=11 April 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200411004717/http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Cassidy included in Ireland squad">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7657596.stm|title=Cassidy included in Ireland squad|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|access-date=7 October 2008}}</ref>

===Rugby union===
Although Graham spent most of his career playing rugby league he did spend several seasons playing [[rugby union]] at northern clubs [[Halifax RUFC|Halifax]] (2002–03) and [[Preston Grasshoppers R.F.C.|Preston Grasshoppers]] (2011–12).<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rolling-maul.com/player-movements-and-signings-in-national-2_topic6242.html|title=Player Movements and signings in national 2|publisher=rolling-maul.com|date=21 June 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugby.statbunker.com/players/GetHistoryStats?player_id=9974&comps_type=-1&dates=-1|title=Graham Holroyd Record by competition|publisher=Rugby Statbunker|date=28 April 2012}}</ref>
